hero.title: Request an accessibility project
hero.lead: IATF reviews requests for accessibility projects that start from a real need.
support: You do not need a full specification. A clear problem, who it affects, and why it matters is enough to start.

intro: All routes enter the same review.

items.label: Website
items.body: Use the form below to submit a structured request.

items.label: Email
items.body: Send the request directly to {{contact.requestsEmail}}. Email requests may become public request records after review and redaction.

items.label: Discord
items.body: Start there if that is easiest. The request still moves into the same review.

guidance.title: Before you send a request
paragraphs: IATF works in public. Requests may be summarized and published as public request records in the IATF GitHub organization, on this website, or both.

Do not include private addresses, phone numbers, medical details, or identifying information about individual users of assistive technologies unless it is necessary and you are comfortable with IATF reviewing it.
IATF removes unnecessary personal contact details before publishing request records.

review.title: Review expectations
paragraphs: Submitting a request does not guarantee acceptance.
IATF reviews requests for mission fit, feasibility, usefulness, and whether the work belongs in a public repository.
Some requests may be declined, deferred, or reshaped before acceptance.

fields.name: projectTitle
fields.label: Project title
fields.helper: A short working title is enough.

fields.name: problem
fields.label: What problem are you trying to solve?
fields.helper: Describe the barrier in plain language.

fields.name: softwareIdea
fields.label: What kind of software do you imagine?

fields.name: references
fields.label: Examples, references, or existing materials
fields.helper: Share anything that helps explain the need.

fields.name: urgency
fields.label: Urgency or timing constraint
fields.helper: Mention deadlines or time constraints if they matter.

fields.name: openSourceConsent
fields.label: Comfortable with public open source development?
fields.helper: Accepted projects are public repositories under IATF.

items: a public repository in the IATF GitHub organization
a responsible engineer assigned to the project
a copyleft license for the code and project materials
a registry entry on the IATF site

header.title: A short, clear request is enough to start

paragraphs: If you have a real accessibility need and think it should become a public project, send it to us.
